page: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is a virtualized server instance powered by the QEMU hypervisor, featuring an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. Memory capacity is set at 8.0 GB, yielding approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 160 GB SSD, and the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 3040.68 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1975.0, both placing in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core performance falls into the average range, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12106.51 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6058.0. Memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is strong at 105.43 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains average at 125.69 GB/sec. Tradeoffs are visible in static web serving throughput, which is weak at 4.05 GB/sec, and medium-model 7B LLM prompt processing, which is also weak at 22.0 tokens/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the cpx32 offers a budget-friendly compute-oriented profile. The instance is suited for workloads that benefit from strong single-thread execution but do not require dedicated CPU cores or high memory density. Appropriate workloads include lightweight software compilation, basic database operations, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference using models under 1B parameters.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server utilizing the QEMU hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It features 4 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a 160 GB local SSD, with no integrated GPU. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1975.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 3040.68, alongside strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 105.43 GB/sec. However, multi-core operations, memory read speeds, and database operations perform in the average tier, while static web serving throughput is weak at 4.05 GB/sec. This resource profile provides a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as text processing, small-scale software compilation, and lightweight utility hosting.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 160 GB SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for budget-conscious compute workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 160 GB SSD for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining AMD Genoa single-core processing strength with local SSD storage for compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB RAM and 160 GB local SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory write performance
Average multi-core and database operation capabilities
Weak static web serving throughput of 4.05 GB/sec
Best suited for lightweight software compilation and text processing
